adds.rationale: Rocket Lab reported record Q3 2022 revenue of $63.1M (+1,093% YoY, +14% QoQ) with three successful Electron missions and $333M cash, a genuine revenue inflection for the rockets/spacecraft wave.

news_evidence.summary: Rocket Lab Q3 2022: record revenue $63.1M (+1,093% YoY); Q4 guidance $51-54M; three successful Electron missions.
CNBC: Rocket Lab record revenue + contract wins; adjusted EBITDA loss improved 62% YoY; Space Systems $40.1M of the $63.1M.

adds.rationale: Russia's 2022 invasion triggered a structural multi-year NATO rearmament cycle; ITA gained ~+10% in CY2022 while the S&P 500 fell ~18%, and the spending uplift is ongoing — opening the watchlist to the profile's geopolitical wave via a liquid established instrument.

news_evidence.summary: Aerospace/defense ETFs surged on the Feb 24, 2022 invasion; ITA/PPA delivered ~+13% vs an S&P 500 decline; Maxar +40% post-invasion.
Defense ETFs (ITA, PPA, XAR) at a one-year high on rising NATO budgets and order backlogs; ITA ~+10% in CY2022, outpacing broad benchmarks.

adds.rationale: The Russia-Ukraine energy shock reversed European nuclear phase-outs and created a structural uranium supply deficit (2022 production ~58,200 MT vs ~60,000 MT demand); URNM, reorganized as a pure-play uranium-miners ETF under Sprott in April 2022, is the cleanest US-listed vehicle for the nuclear wave.

news_evidence.summary: WEF (Feb 2022): nuclear-fuel supply concern spiked as Russia's enrichment control created vulnerability; countries signaled reversal of phase-out policies.
Sprott reorganized the North Shore uranium ETF as the Sprott Uranium Miners ETF (URNM) on April 25, 2022 — companies with >=50% assets in uranium mining.

rationale_overall: The Q4 2022 rebalance opens the portfolio to three non-AI waves with active 2022 structural catalysts: RKLB (rockets, Q3 2022 revenue +1,093% YoY), ITA (geopolitical rearmament, ~28pp of 2022 outperformance vs the S&P 500), and URNM (nuclear, uranium supply-deficit and energy-security narrative set off by the same geopolitical shock). The AI bucket (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA) stays at its four-ticker max and SPY remains the broad-market anchor; the three adds bring the watchlist to its 8-ticker ceiling spanning five declared waves.
